hero.headline: AI Powered Healthcare Assistant for Rural Communities
hero.subtext: Understand symptoms, get medical guidance, and connect to healthcare resources — in your own language.

features.title: How We Help
features.symptom_checker_desc: Describe your symptoms in any supported language and get instant analysis of possible conditions.
features.multilingual_desc: Available in English, Telugu, Hindi, and Tamil — breaking language barriers in healthcare.
features.voice_desc: Speak your symptoms naturally. Our voice assistant understands and responds in your language.
features.health_awareness_desc: Access curated health education content about common diseases, prevention, and hygiene.

how_it_works.title: How It Works
how_it_works.step1_desc: Type or speak your symptoms in your preferred language.
how_it_works.step2_desc: Our AI engine processes your symptoms and matches them against medical knowledge.
how_it_works.step3_desc: Receive possible conditions, severity level, precautions, and when to visit a doctor.

symptom.title: Symptom Analysis
symptom.subtitle: Describe your symptoms and get AI-powered medical guidance
symptom.input_placeholder: Describe your symptoms... (e.g., fever and headache)
symptom.welcome_message: Hello! I'm your AI health assistant. Describe your symptoms and I'll help you understand what might be going on. Please remember, this is not a substitute for professional medical advice.
symptom.disclaimer: ⚠️ This tool provides general health information only. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider for medical advice.

voice_page.title: Voice Assistant
voice_page.subtitle: Speak your symptoms and get instant health guidance
voice_page.instruction: Press the microphone button and describe your symptoms in your language. The AI will analyze and respond.

education.title: Health Education
education.subtitle: Learn about common health conditions, prevention, and hygiene practices

topics.fever_desc: Fever is a temporary increase in body temperature. Learn when it's serious and basic remedies.
fever_facts: Normal body temperature is around 36.1°C to 37.2°C (97°F to 99°F)
Fever is generally defined as a temperature above 38°C (100.4°F)
Fever itself is not a disease — it is a symptom of an underlying condition
Most fevers are caused by infections such as cold, flu, or COVID-19
Children under 5 are more vulnerable to high fevers
fever_prevention: Wash hands frequently with soap and water
Avoid close contact with sick individuals
Keep living spaces clean and well-ventilated
Ensure proper nutrition to maintain immunity
fever_treatment: Rest and drink plenty of fluids (water, ORS, juices)
Use a damp cloth on the forehead to reduce temperature
Take paracetamol as directed (not aspirin for children)
topics.fever_doctor: Seek medical help if fever exceeds 103°F (39.4°C), lasts more than 3 days, is accompanied by severe headache, stiff neck, rash, difficulty breathing, or persistent vomiting.
topics.hygiene_desc: Simple hygiene practices that prevent many diseases. Handwashing, clean water, and sanitation.
hygiene_facts: Handwashing with soap can reduce diarrheal diseases by 30% (WHO)
2.3 billion people worldwide lack basic sanitation facilities
Contaminated water causes 485,000 diarrheal deaths each year
Proper hygiene prevents spread of respiratory infections by up to 20%
Open defecation leads to spread of cholera, typhoid, and hepatitis
hygiene_prevention: Wash hands with soap for at least 20 seconds before eating and after using the toilet
Use clean, safe drinking water — boil water if unsure of quality
Keep food covered and stored properly to prevent contamination
Bathe daily and wear clean clothes
Trim nails regularly and keep surroundings clean
hygiene_treatment: If suffering from a hygiene-related illness, use ORS for dehydration
Maintain fluid intake with clean water
Isolate infected individuals to prevent spread
topics.hygiene_doctor: Visit a doctor if you experience persistent diarrhea, bloody stool, high fever with rash, or any skin infection that worsens over time.
topics.nutrition_desc: Balanced diet essentials for rural communities. Affordable and locally available nutritious foods.
nutrition_facts: Undernutrition causes 45% of deaths in children under 5
Iron deficiency is the most common nutritional deficiency worldwide
A balanced diet includes carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, and minerals
Exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months provides ideal nutrition for infants
nutrition_prevention: Eat a variety of foods including grains, lentils, vegetables, and fruits
Include protein sources: dal, eggs, milk, soy, and fish
Eat iron-rich foods: green leafy vegetables, jaggery, dates
Ensure pregnant women take iron and folic acid supplements
Feed children nutrient-rich complementary foods after 6 months
nutrition_treatment: For malnourished children: provide energy-dense foods like khichdi with ghee
Give ORS and zinc supplements during diarrhea
Include Vitamin A rich foods: carrots, papaya, mango, sweet potato
topics.nutrition_doctor: See a doctor if a child shows signs of severe malnutrition: visible wasting, swollen feet/face, hair changes, extreme weakness, or failure to grow.
topics.first_aid_desc: Essential first aid knowledge for common injuries, burns, and emergencies.
first_aid_facts: Unintentional injuries are among the top causes of death in rural areas
Prompt first aid can prevent an injury from becoming life-threatening
Burns are classified into 3 degrees based on severity
Snakebites cause approximately 81,000-138,000 deaths per year globally (WHO)
Keeping a basic first aid kit at home can save lives
first_aid_prevention: Keep sharp objects and chemicals away from children
Use protective footwear to prevent snakebites and injuries
Store medicines and cleaning agents separately
Ensure proper lighting in homes and pathways
Learn basic CPR and choking relief techniques
first_aid_treatment: For cuts: Clean with clean water, apply antiseptic, cover with clean cloth
For burns: Cool with running water for 10-20 minutes, do not apply ice or toothpaste
For snakebite: Keep patient calm, immobilize the limb, rush to hospital — do NOT tie tightly or cut the wound
For choking: Perform back blows and abdominal thrusts
For fractures: Immobilize the area and seek medical help immediately
topics.first_aid_doctor: Seek emergency medical help for deep wounds, severe burns, animal/snake bites, head injuries, fractures, persistent bleeding, or unconsciousness.
topics.maternal_desc: Important health practices during pregnancy, childbirth, and postnatal care.
maternal_facts: Approximately 810 women die daily from preventable pregnancy-related causes (WHO)
Most maternal deaths occur in low-resource settings and are preventable
At least 4 antenatal care visits are recommended during pregnancy
Skilled birth attendants can reduce maternal mortality significantly
Postnatal care within 24 hours is critical for mother and newborn
maternal_prevention: Start antenatal check-ups in the first trimester
Take iron, folic acid, and calcium supplements as prescribed
Get Tetanus Toxoid (TT) vaccination during pregnancy
Eat nutritious food: green vegetables, milk, eggs, fruits
Avoid heavy lifting, tobacco, alcohol, and self-medication
Plan for delivery at a health facility with skilled attendants
maternal_treatment: For morning sickness: eat small, frequent meals; ginger tea may help
For swollen feet: elevate legs and reduce salt intake
Initiate breastfeeding within 1 hour of birth
Keep the newborn warm and ensure exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months
topics.maternal_doctor: Seek immediate medical help for vaginal bleeding, severe headache with blurred vision, high fever, convulsions, reduced fetal movement, or water breaking before due date.
topics.waterborne_desc: Prevention and treatment of diseases caused by contaminated water sources.
waterborne_facts: Waterborne diseases cause an estimated 3.4 million deaths per year (WHO)
Common waterborne diseases: Cholera, Typhoid, Hepatitis A, Dysentery
Unsafe water, sanitation, and hygiene cause 60% of diarrheal disease burden
Children under 5 are most vulnerable to waterborne diseases
Contaminated water is a major issue during floods and monsoon season
waterborne_prevention: Always boil or filter water before drinking
Store water in clean, covered containers
Wash hands thoroughly before eating and after using the toilet
Use chlorine tablets or water purifiers where available
Avoid eating raw food washed with unclean water
Ensure proper drainage and avoid stagnant water near homes
waterborne_treatment: For diarrhea: Give ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution) immediately
Continue feeding — do not stop food during diarrhea
Give zinc supplements to children with diarrhea for 10-14 days
For cholera symptoms: rehydrate aggressively and seek medical help
topics.waterborne_doctor: Seek medical help immediately for watery diarrhea with vomiting, blood in stool, high fever with diarrhea, signs of severe dehydration (sunken eyes, dry mouth, no urination), or symptoms lasting more than 2 days.

emergency.title: Emergency Helpline Numbers
emergency.subtitle: Important numbers to call during medical emergencies in India
emergency.note: These numbers are toll-free and available 24/7 across India.

numbers.name: National Emergency Number
numbers.desc: Single emergency number for Police, Fire, and Ambulance

numbers.name: Ambulance / Emergency Medical
numbers.desc: Free emergency ambulance service available across most states

numbers.name: Health Helpline (Govt. of India)
numbers.desc: Health advice, disease information, and hospital directory

numbers.name: COVID-19 Helpline
numbers.desc: Dedicated helpline for COVID-19 related queries and assistance

numbers.name: Women Helpline
numbers.desc: Women in distress can call for immediate help and support

numbers.name: Child Helpline
numbers.desc: For children in need of care and protection

numbers.name: Mental Health Helpline (NIMHANS)
numbers.desc: Free mental health counseling and support

numbers.name: Blood Bank (Red Cross)
numbers.desc: Toll-free number for blood bank information and requests

numbers.name: TB Helpline
numbers.desc: For tuberculosis information, testing, and treatment support

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Bringing AI-powered healthcare to underserved communities
about.mission_desc: We believe everyone deserves access to basic health information, regardless of language or location. Our AI Rural Healthcare Assistant bridges the gap between rural communities and medical knowledge.
about.how_desc: Using advanced AI, we analyze symptoms, provide basic medical guidance in multiple languages, and help users understand when professional medical care is needed.
about.disclaimer_desc: This application is designed for informational purposes only. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of a qualified healthcare provider.

footer.description: AI-powered healthcare assistant for rural and migrant communities.
footer.disclaimer_text: This tool provides general health information only. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice.

ngo.title: NGO Healthcare Dashboard
ngo.map_note: Hover over dots to view region details
ngo.name: Name
ngo.notification_desc: Configure alert thresholds and notification preferences for health monitoring.

ngo_onboarding.title: NGO Profile Setup
ngo_onboarding.subtitle: Set up your organization profile to get started

ai_settings.title: AI Model Settings
ai_settings.description: Choose which AI model to use for symptom analysis. Different models offer trade-offs between speed and accuracy.
ai_settings.gemini_flash_desc: Fast and balanced AI model. Good accuracy with low latency.
ai_settings.gemini_pro_desc: Highest accuracy model. Best for complex symptom analysis. Slower response.
ai_settings.gpt5_desc: Powerful reasoning model. Excellent for nuanced medical analysis.
ai_settings.fine_tuned_desc: Custom fine-tuned model for rural healthcare. Requires external endpoint configuration.
ai_settings.no_data: No evaluation data yet. Metrics will appear after symptom analyses are performed.
ai_settings.fine_tune_info: To use a custom fine-tuned model (e.g., Mistral 7B with LoRA), deploy it externally and configure the endpoint:
ai_settings.fine_tune_note: The endpoint must accept OpenAI-compatible chat completion requests.

login.subtitle: Sign in to access health services
login.disclaimer: By signing in, you agree that this tool provides general health information only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice.

precautions.rest_quiet: Rest in a quiet, dark room
precautions.rice_banana: Eat light foods like rice and banana

when_to_visit_texts.fever: If fever persists for more than 3 days or exceeds 103°F (39.4°C)
when_to_visit_texts.headache: If headache is severe, sudden, or accompanied by vision changes
when_to_visit_texts.cough: If cough persists for more than 2 weeks or produces blood
when_to_visit_texts.stomach: If pain is severe, persistent, or accompanied by blood in stool
when_to_visit_texts.diarrhea: If diarrhea persists for more than 2 days or signs of dehydration appear
when_to_visit_texts.bodyache: If body ache is accompanied by high fever, rash, or joint swelling
when_to_visit_texts.default: If symptoms persist or worsen, please visit a doctor.
